published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024: general automated sharp bounds for discrete causal questions; BalkePearl1997: static binary-IV LP

repo_prior_art.cite: API section 3 plus pid_late_bounded_defiers: only static/one-period partial-ID endpoints are adjacent

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997 gives the T=1 static binary-IV bound; exact-compliance endpoint is standard sequential IPW

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_late_bounded_defiers: one-period complier-LATE envelope; no T=3 full-history dynamic IV proposal found

published_prior_art.cite: closest: ChenZhang2023, Han2024, MichaelCuiLorchTchetgen2024, VanDenBergBonevMammen2020; none states strict non-Markov full-history tightening at fixed stagewise margins
closest: BalkePearl1997 static sharpness and ChenZhang2023 Bellman partial-ID; neither gives a no-pasting theorem for one joint dynamic response map

repo_prior_art.cite: ExactID/DynamicLATE is two-period exact-ID under stronger assumptions, not a T<=2 partial-ID impossibility/minimality statement

published_prior_art.cite: none found among verified dynamic IV, sustained-IV-null, and OPE papers

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 | Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The related-work comparison should add Duarte et al. 2024, 'An Automated Approach to Causal Inference in Discrete Settings', because it gives general sharp-bound machinery for discrete causal models with noncompliance and is the closest generic published competitor to the proposed finite LP object.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The statement uses 'history-specific static Balke-Pearl problem' and 'interval recursion' without defining the exact one-period observed law, latent table, and compatibility map being pasted.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The phrase 'quotienting duplicate histories and zero-probability assignment cells' is not formalized, so the T<=2 impossibility statement lacks a precise equivalence relation/search space.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define the stagewise Balke-Pearl subproblem in Conjecture 2 as an explicit family of local LPs and add the linear marginalization map whose failure gives the Farkas certificate.

Add Duarte et al. 2024/autobounds to Related work and explain that the proposal's novelty is an analytic non-Markov history-separation theorem, not merely the existence of a generic discrete sharp-bound program.
Replace the informal quotient language in Conjecture 3 with a finite canonicalization rule for histories and a precise T<=2 enumeration target.
Produce a small rational primal-dual LP certificate for Conjecture 1, even as an appendix sketch, to separate strict width reduction from the stronger sign-relevant version.

recommended_next_step: Revise definitions for Conjectures 2-3 and add the missing generic sharp-bound literature comparison; no pivot is needed.
